replies.author: @marymcelroy6157
replies.text: @charizard4410 There's marine snow, which is basically flakes of organic material drifting down to the aphotic zones to serve as sustenance for the critters that live down there, and dead whales contribute to that; the body sinks to the seafloor as a whale fall and organic fragments peel off here and there and sort of descend too

replies.author: @tin2001
replies.text:
I went on a behind the scenes tour at a major conservation zoo... At one stage we were in a non-public area they keep some of the elephants for different reasons, including when they're sick or just getting old and frail. And they told us how they bury them when they do go... An excavator is one tool they use. And a chainsaw is another. They said there's not really a practical way to bury them whole....
And I tell that story because every single time I see one of those superfats, I always think of that little part of the zoo out the back where most people don't see.

replies.author: @nikkidarkangelpnope8400
replies.text: ​@tin2001 There was a story in the news here this week about a woman who is trying to get the government to change the regulations for crematoriums because she couldn't find one to cremate her 715 lb father. I mean, I understand it was difficult for her but we don't need the government to normalize this.

replies.author: @marymcelroy6157
replies.text: @tin2001But the elephants you feel at least some compassion and sadness for, wonderful beasts, highly intelligent and just interesting in general.

replies.author: @donnerblitzen1388
replies.text:
​@party4lifedude

Hate to say this, but I knew a few people years ago who actually said something similar to that exact phrase. It was:
"I have cash, I want to ruin myself on meth, let's call the plug"

When I heard that, I left the building, because it always led to trouble. As of now, one of those last I heard is homeless and quite possibly insane due to mental issues because of that addiction and the other lost his life this past December. 

What a world we live in, huh?

replies.author: @donnerblitzen1388
replies.text: The liquid creamer hurts my stomach, I prefer the powdered stuff but only in small amounts.  I know that isn't really good for me, but I don't treat myself very much anymore.  I don't even eat cookies or chips now. I rarely drink sodas even. So, some creamer in my coffee in the morning is great. When I get some coffee at a shop (local store, not a chain place), it's always black and sometimes it's flavored but never with syrup. Not my thing anymore.

replies.author: @dramspringfeald
replies.text:
​@SueP-Dit's more of how it's grown through the meat. And when cooked it renders into the fiber at an interesting way, it's really good. (Mine had a little bit of pepper, salt and garlic powder, so good) 

ground beef is just a mixture of fat and beef fed into a grinder and handed out as is.

author: @txorado8203
text: I remember working at Starbucks and this one regular would come in and get  a grande white mocha in a venti cup with  20 pumps of white mocha made with heavy whipping cream and he finished it off with whipped cream and caramel sauce on top. He looked like he was easily 500lbs. He was so heavy Ive never seen shoes so thin. Fast forward, as a first responder We had to cut a wall off this pts house and use a crane to get him out. The bariatric unit couldn’t hold him so we had to get a box truck to carry him to the hospital. No excuse to get that big ever!
